description: Create the apps/quantcalendar workspace package with all backend services, routes, and tests for QuantCalendar - a standalone calendar app with AI scheduling capabilities.
steps: Step 1 - Register quantcalendar in the ecosystem: Add 'quantcalendar' to the QuantApp union type in packages/common/src/types.ts. Add the quantcalendar entry in packages/common/src/constants.ts QUANT_APPS record with name 'QuantCalendar', description 'Calendar with AI scheduling, booking links, and smart time management', color '#FF8C00'.

Step 2 - Create apps/quantcalendar/package.json following the pattern in apps/quantdrive/package.json. Name: @quant/quantcalendar. Dependencies: @quant/auth, @quant/ai, @quant/common, @quant/database, @quant/realtime, @quant/server-core, @quant/shared-ui (all workspace:*), fastify ^5.3.3, ioredis ^5.6.1, zod ^3.25.23, uuid ^9.0.0. DevDeps: @types/uuid ^9, vitest ^2. Scripts: test -> vitest run, typecheck -> tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.backend.json.

Step 3 - Create apps/quantcalendar/tsconfig.json extending ../../tsconfig.json with outDir ./dist, rootDir ., jsx react-jsx. Include src/**/*.ts, src/**/*.tsx, backend/**/*.ts. References to packages auth, ai, common, database, realtime, server-core, shared-ui.

Step 4 - Create apps/quantcalendar/tsconfig.backend.json following the exact pattern in apps/quantdrive/tsconfig.backend.json. Set strict: true, noEmit: true, isolatedModules: true, paths to all referenced packages.
Step 5 - Create apps/quantcalendar/vitest.config.ts: defineConfig with test.globals: true, test.environment: node, test.include: ['backend/__tests__/**/*.test.ts'].

Step 6 - Create backend/services/event.service.ts: Define CalendarEvent interface (id, title, description, startTime: Date, endTime: Date, allDay: boolean, location, userId, attendees: Attendee[], recurrenceRule: string|null, status: 'confirmed'|'tentative'|'cancelled', reminders: Reminder[], createdAt, updatedAt). Define Attendee interface (userId, email, name, status: 'accepted'|'declined'|'tentative'|'pending'). Define Reminder interface (type: 'email'|'push'|'sms', minutesBefore: number). Define PrismaClient interface for event model. Implement EventService class with: createEvent(input), getEvent(eventId, userId), updateEvent(eventId, userId, input), deleteEvent(eventId, userId), listEvents(userId, startDate, endDate), addAttendee(eventId, userId, attendee), removeAttendee(eventId, userId, attendeeUserId), updateAttendeeStatus(eventId, attendeeUserId, status).

Step 7 - Create backend/services/recurring.service.ts: Implement RecurringService class that handles RRULE expansion. Interface RecurrenceRule (frequency: 'daily'|'weekly'|'monthly'|'yearly', interval: number, count?: number, until?: Date, byDay?: string[], byMonth?: number[], exceptions?: Date[]). Methods: expandRecurrence(event, startRange, endRange) -> CalendarEvent[] (generates occurrences), addException(eventId, userId, exceptionDate), parseRRule(rruleString) -> RecurrenceRule, serializeRRule(rule) -> string. Implement the RRULE expansion logic manually (do NOT use external rrule library) with proper handling of daily/weekly/monthly/yearly patterns.

Step 8 - Create backend/services/availability.service.ts: Implement AvailabilityService. Methods: getAvailability(userId, date, workingHoursStart, workingHoursEnd) -> TimeSlot[] (free slots), checkMultiUserAvailability(userIds: string[], date, duration) -> TimeSlot[] (common free slots). Interface TimeSlot (start: Date, end: Date). Uses PrismaClient to query events for given users/dates.

Step 9 - Create backend/services/ai-schedule.service.ts: Implement AIScheduleService (takes AIEngine + PrismaClient). Method: suggestMeetingTimes(organizerUserId, attendeeIds: string[], duration: number, preferences?: SchedulePreferences) -> SuggestedSlot[]. Interface SchedulePreferences (preferMorning?: boolean, preferAfternoon?: boolean, avoidLunchHour?: boolean). Interface SuggestedSlot (start: Date, end: Date, score: number, reason: string). Uses AvailabilityService internally to find common free slots, then uses AI to rank and explain the best options.

Step 10 - Create backend/services/ai-buffer.service.ts: Implement AIBufferService. Method: addBufferTime(userId, date) -> BufferResult. Scans events for the given day, identifies back-to-back meetings (gap < 5 min), inserts 15-minute buffer events between them. Interface BufferResult (buffersAdded: number, events: CalendarEvent[]). Uses PrismaClient to read events and create buffer events.

Step 11 - Create backend/services/ai-focus-blocks.service.ts: Implement AIFocusBlocksService (takes AIEngine + PrismaClient). Method: reserveFocusBlocks(userId, date, minBlockMinutes?: number) -> FocusBlockResult. Scans the day for empty slots >= minBlockMinutes (default 60), creates 'Deep Work' events in those slots. Interface FocusBlockResult (blocksCreated: number, events: CalendarEvent[]). Uses AI to pick the best slots based on the user's typical schedule patterns.

Step 12 - Create backend/services/ai-cancel-detector.service.ts: Implement AICancelDetectorService (takes AIEngine). Method: detectCancellation(messageText: string) -> CancelDetectionResult. Interface CancelDetectionResult (isCancellation: boolean, confidence: number, suggestedAction: 'reschedule'|'cancel'|'none', extractedReason?: string). Uses AI to analyze text for cancellation intent patterns like 'I can't make it', 'need to cancel', 'won't be able to attend'.

Step 13 - Create backend/services/ai-reschedule.service.ts: Implement AIRescheduleService (takes AIEngine + PrismaClient). Method: rescheduleEvents(userId, instruction: string) -> RescheduleResult. Parses natural language like 'Move my Thursday meetings to Friday' using AI, identifies affected events, finds available slots on the target day, returns proposed changes. Interface RescheduleResult (proposedChanges: ProposedChange[], parsed: { sourceDay: string, targetDay: string }). Interface ProposedChange (eventId: string, originalStart: Date, originalEnd: Date, newStart: Date, newEnd: Date).

Step 14 - Create backend/services/ai-weekly-digest.service.ts: Implement AIWeeklyDigestService (takes AIEngine + PrismaClient). Method: generateDigest(userId, weekStartDate: Date) -> WeeklyDigest. Interface WeeklyDigest (summary: string, totalMeetings: number, totalHoursBooked: number, busiestDay: string, suggestions: string[]). Fetches the week's events, computes stats, uses AI to generate a friendly summary and productivity suggestions.

Step 15 - Create backend/services/booking-link.service.ts: Implement BookingLinkService (takes PrismaClient). Interfaces: BookingLink (id, userId, slug, title, description, duration: number, availableDays: number[], startHour: number, endHour: number, isActive: boolean, createdAt, updatedAt), BookingSlot (start: Date, end: Date, available: boolean). Methods: createBookingLink(input), getBookingLink(slug), getAvailableSlots(slug, date) -> BookingSlot[], confirmBooking(slug, slot: Date, bookerInfo: BookerInfo). Interface BookerInfo (name: string, email: string, notes?: string). Uses availability logic to compute open slots.

Step 16 - Create backend/routes/events.ts: Fastify route plugin with prefix assumed /api/v1/events. Endpoints: POST / (create event), GET / (list events with query params start/end dates), GET /:id (get event), PUT /:id (update event), DELETE /:id (delete event), POST /:id/attendees (add attendee), DELETE /:id/attendees/:attendeeId (remove attendee), PATCH /:id/attendees/:attendeeId/status (update attendee status). Use zod for request validation. Follow the pattern in apps/quantdrive/backend/routes/files.ts.

Step 17 - Create backend/routes/booking.ts: Fastify route plugin. Endpoints: POST /links (create booking link), GET /links/:slug (get booking link info), GET /links/:slug/slots (get available slots for date query param), POST /links/:slug/book (confirm booking). Use zod validation.

Step 18 - Create backend/routes/ai.ts: Fastify route plugin. Endpoints: POST /suggest-times (AI schedule suggestions), POST /buffer (add buffer time), POST /focus-blocks (reserve focus blocks), POST /detect-cancellation (detect cancellation in text), POST /reschedule (reschedule events by instruction), GET /weekly-digest (get weekly digest). Use zod validation.

Step 19 - Create backend/app.ts: Following the exact pattern of apps/quantdrive/backend/app.ts. Export getConfig() and buildApp(). Register routes: eventsRoutes at /api/v1/events, bookingRoutes at /api/v1/booking, aiRoutes at /api/v1/ai. Port 3060, jwtIssuer 'quantcalendar'.

Step 20 - Create backend/__tests__/event.service.test.ts: Test EventService CRUD operations, attendee management, and status updates. Use mock PrismaClient following the pattern in apps/quantdrive/backend/__tests__/file.service.test.ts. Cover: create event with attendees, get event, update event, delete event, add/remove attendees, update attendee status, error cases (not found, unauthorized).

Step 21 - Create backend/__tests__/recurring.service.test.ts: Test RecurringService RRULE expansion. Cover: daily recurrence (expand 7 days), weekly recurrence with byDay, monthly recurrence, yearly recurrence, recurrence with count limit, recurrence with until date, exceptions skipped, interval > 1.

Step 22 - Create backend/__tests__/availability.service.test.ts: Test AvailabilityService. Cover: returns free slots when events are scattered, returns empty when fully booked, multi-user availability finds common gaps.

Step 23 - Create backend/__tests__/ai-schedule.service.test.ts: Test AIScheduleService. Mock AIEngine.infer to return JSON suggestions. Cover: suggests available times across multiple calendars, respects preferences (morning/afternoon), returns scored and ranked slots.

Step 24 - Create backend/__tests__/ai-buffer.service.test.ts: Test AIBufferService. Cover: adds buffers between back-to-back meetings, does nothing when no back-to-back meetings exist, handles edge case of meetings separated by exactly 5 minutes.

Step 25 - Create backend/__tests__/ai-focus-blocks.service.test.ts: Test AIFocusBlocksService. Mock AI. Cover: reserves blocks in empty 60+ minute gaps, respects minBlockMinutes parameter, does nothing when day is fully packed.

Step 26 - Create backend/__tests__/ai-cancel-detector.service.test.ts: Test AICancelDetectorService. Mock AI. Cover: detects 'I can't make it' as cancellation, detects 'need to cancel' as cancellation, does not flag normal messages.

Step 27 - Create backend/__tests__/ai-reschedule.service.test.ts: Test AIRescheduleService. Mock AI to return parsed instruction. Cover: parses 'Move Thursday meetings to Friday', returns proposed changes with correct new times.

Step 28 - Create backend/__tests__/ai-weekly-digest.service.test.ts: Test AIWeeklyDigestService. Mock AI and PrismaClient. Cover: generates digest with correct stats (total meetings, hours), includes AI-generated summary.

Step 29 - Create backend/__tests__/booking-link.service.test.ts: Test BookingLinkService. Cover: create booking link, get by slug, get available slots (shows booked slots as unavailable), confirm booking creates event.
Step 30 - Run pnpm install from repo root to link the new workspace package, then run NODE_OPTIONS="" npx vitest run to verify all tests pass.
acceptance_criteria: apps/quantcalendar/ workspace package exists with proper package.json, tsconfigs, and vitest config
RecurringService expands RRULE patterns (daily/weekly/monthly/yearly) with exceptions correctly
AIScheduleService suggests available times across multiple calendars
AIBufferService adds 15-min buffers between back-to-back meetings
AIFocusBlocksService reserves deep work blocks in empty time slots
AICancelDetectorService detects cancellation language with confidence score
AIRescheduleService parses natural language reschedule instructions
AIWeeklyDigestService generates weekly summary with stats and suggestions
BookingLinkService provides Calendly-like public booking with available slots
AvailabilityService checks availability across multiple users
All routes (events, booking, ai) are properly defined with zod validation
quantcalendar is registered in packages/common/src/types.ts QuantApp type and constants.ts
NODE_OPTIONS="" npx vitest run passes with all new tests
verification: Run: NODE_OPTIONS="" npx vitest run from /projects/sandbox/Quant-Ecosystem - all tests must pass including new quantcalendar tests
findings: All 44 quantcalendar tests pass. Full monorepo suite passes (271 test files, 2538 tests). Required NODE_OPTIONS="" for git commit due to husky hook issue. Prettier lint-staged ran successfully on all files.
